key_facts: MARKET PAIR DEFINITION (canonical, 'Shall We Vote on Values, But Bet on Beliefs?', 2000/2007/2013, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/futarchy.pdf, published version https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/futarchy2013.pdf): 'A financial house agreed to ensure that there are always dump-the-OldTek-CEO stock markets, trading OldTek stock conditional on dumping the current CEO at the end of the current quarter... these agreements to trade would be called off (i.e., made null and void) if the CEO were not dumped by the end of the current quarter.' Payoff numeraire = the company's stock itself vs cash; the conditional price IS the expected stock value under that regime.

TOKENIZED EQUIVALENT Hanson himself gives (footnote 6, same paper): 'Alternatively, the assets could be contingent instead of the trades. That is, assets of the form "Pays $1 if CEO dumped" could be traded for assets of the form "Pays one OldTek stock if CEO dumped".' This is exactly the Gnosis-CTF/futarchy-fi conditional-token construction — split collateral into IF-DUMPED/IF-KEPT legs, void (full-refund/merge) the leg whose condition fails.

DECISION RULE (same paper, verbatim): 'The OldTek corporate charter might say that if the dump-the-CEO price were clearly higher than the keep-the-CEO stock price, the board must dump him within the next quarter. (The price might be deemed "clearly higher" if the dump-the-CEO bid price were above the keep-the-CEO ask price for 90% of the last week of a quarter.)' — i.e., bid-vs-ask dominance over a sustained averaging window, not an instantaneous mid-price crossing.

DECISION RULE 2024 REFINEMENT (Futarchy Details, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/futarchy-details): 'Make a statistical model of the two prices over/near the decision period' and approve only if the model is 'confident enough' the period-end price difference isn't chance; plus 'extra strong liquidity subsidy just before the period' when the price difference is measured; agenda via auctioning regular proposal slots.

ADVISORY MODE PROTOCOL (Advisory Futarchy, 2025-05-11, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/advisory-futarchy): futarchy 'could advise whether to keep or dump the CEO'; Condition A: declare the decision timeframe in advance and use market price AVERAGES over a pre-set window ('48 and 24 hours seem sufficient'); Condition B: 'Allow and encourage key decision makers and advisors who have access to inside info relevant to the decision to trade in the markets'; subsidize the run-up period; decision makers start advisory and 'slowly put more weight on them as they proved themselves via concrete track records'.

ORIGINAL 1996 PROPOSAL (Markets for Dumping CEOs, 1996-04-26, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/dumpceo.html, verbatim): 'I propose to create, for each stock, a separate market in that stock for trades which are "called-off" if the CEO does not step down in the next year... If that stock price is consistently and significantly higher than the ordinary stock price, that should be a clear market signal, from informed traders, for the CEO to step down. (If there is no price, because there is no trading, then there is no signal.)' Users: boards ('board members may more plausibly fear legal action for ignoring these market signals') and shame-publicity in the 'Just Vote No' campaign context where worst companies 'are then publicized' — the seed of the ranking/scoreboard idea. Note: 1996 compares dump-conditional vs ORDINARY spot price with a 1-year horizon; from 2000 on he uses the explicit dump/keep pair at quarter horizon.

FORMAL MATH (Decision Market Math, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/decision-market-mathhtml): with A = 'CEO stays through quarter end', split $1 into '$1 if A' + '$1 if not A'; x1=E[x|A] from trading '$x if A' against '$1 if A', x2=E[x|not A] likewise; CEO value-added = x1-x2.

SUBSIDIES ARE MANDATORY: 1999 IEEE Intelligent Systems 'Decision Markets' (https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/decisionmarkets.pdf): 'a subsidized market can function over any time period with only one trader'; subsidy 'in effect creates an information prize'; Futarchy Details: 'Better to just subsidize these markets... net subsidies on average roughly a third of the value that proposals add.' The subsidized automated market maker is his own invention — LMSR (Logarithmic Market Scoring Rules, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/mktscore.pdf; also 'Combinatorial Information Market Design' 2003).

MANIPULATION RESISTANCE (futarchy paper + experiments): 'on average interested parties cannot bias the price; they can at worst only add noise'; manipulators are just noise traders that increase returns to informed trading — cites Hanson & Oprea 'Manipulators increase information market accuracy' and Hanson/Oprea/Porter JEBO 2006, Camerer 1998. Insider-trading design: guarantee ANONYMITY for non-insiders (the 'Disgruntled Jones' junior employee can bet against his CEO without retribution); require the CEO's (and insiders') trades to be identified and his compensation public.

DECISION SELECTION BIAS — the conditional!=causal trap (Decision Selection Bias, 2024-12-28, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/decision-selection-bias; also 'Correlation Is Not Causation' section of the futarchy paper): if the board knows more than traders, prices conditional on dump/keep are biased. Fixes he specifies: (1) reveal decision-maker info to speculators or let those holding it trade; (2) 'clearly inform speculators that a decision is about to be made' in a short pre-announced window; (3) commit to randomization — e.g., act on prices conditional on dice='snake eyes'; (4) 'randomly reject say 5% of the changes that it would otherwise have accepted' so the not-adopt condition stays measurable. A real product should implement the random-veto and announced-decision-window mechanics.

WHO USES IT (Hanania interview, 2021-09-15, https://www.richardhanania.com/p/futarchy-robin-hanson-on-how-prediction): boards are the primary audience ('look at that price difference and say, Should we keep him, or should we dump him?'); mechanism extends to ranking alternative CEO candidates and cascading down to CFO/regional managers/ad agencies. Regulatory limit: conditional stock markets are a stock derivative needing SEC/CFTC permission; the deeper barrier is organizational — the markets are 'a very knowledgeable autist in the C suite' that 'will often say things that people do not want to hear'.

WHY PAST ATTEMPTS FAILED, PER HANSON (Prediction Markets Update, 2017-09-19, https://www.overcomingbias.com/2017/09/prediction-markets-update.html + Hanania interview): Augur/Gnosis were 'general platforms, expecting others to fill in the specific betting topics and to do the marketing'; crypto teams were 'software people and algorithm people' not 'business people who work with particular clients'; corporate-adjacent products 'keep the appearance of markets but undercut their main incentive benefits' by excluding insider-relevant topics; combinatorial/subsidized mechanisms remain an untried 'overhang'. Actual 2016 Gnosis futarchy experiments (Ethereum Foundation grant to Liston/Koppelmann, incl. a hire/fire-CEO test) never became a product (https://medium.com/@consensus/an-introduction-to-cryptoeconomics-and-futarchy-experiments-on-gnosis-df85220f840a, https://www.charterless.com/p/a-3b-bet-on-predicting-the-future); GnosisDAO futarchy (2020) stayed advisory-internal (https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2020/11/23/new-gnosisdao-bets-on-futarchy-a-prediction-market-governance-model).

CURRENT STATE PER HANSON (My Best Idea: Decision Markets, 2026-04-25, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/my-best-idea-decision-markets): he names the live trials as MetaDAO and Futarchy.fi (i.e., this team's own stack); 'Most all innovations combine simple elegant ideas with messy details' needing 'concrete trials and experiments'. He became a paid MetaDAO adviser Feb 2025 — hired via a futarchy market on his own hiring (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/02/13/metadao-hires-futarchy-s-creator-robin-hanson-as-advisor). Power Futarchy (2026-04-21, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/power-futarchy): profit-futarchy stalls because 'top people, like the CEO, often do not see their personal success as maxed by firm success' — CEOs block it; implication: a fire-the-CEO product should serve outside audiences (boards under pressure, activists, media), not wait for CEO consent.

RANKING CAVEAT (epistemic flag): Hanson explicitly proposed a market 'for each stock' (1996) and endorsed publicizing worst-governed companies (Just Vote No context), and in interviews says media/boards should read the price gaps; but no verbatim Hanson quote proposing a single 'rank all public companies by fire premium' scoreboard was found in this sweep. The scoreboard is a faithful extrapolation (he framed the signal as comparable across firms and complementary to published shame-rankings), not a documented spec item.
requirements: For Hanson to say 'this is my vision, done right', a ceo.futarchy.fi product must include: (1) PAIRED conditional markets per company — stock-value-if-CEO-out-by-T vs stock-value-if-CEO-in-at-T — implemented either as called-off trades or, per his own footnote, as conditional tokens ('pays $1 if dumped' vs 'pays one share if dumped'), with the failing condition's leg fully voided/refunded, never settled; the futarchy-fi/Gnosis-CTF split-merge-redeem machinery is a direct match here and is reusable as-is. (2) A concrete outcome: (proxy for) stock price at a fixed date T+delta after the horizon, with the CEO-departure condition resolved by an objective oracle definition (departure announced/effective by T — must be pinned precisely, since resignations vs firings blur it). (3) A published, pre-announced measurement window and decision rule — his canonical spec is 'dump-side bid > keep-side ask for 90% of the last week of the quarter', modernized to a time-averaged gap ('fire premium') with statistical confidence, not spot mid-prices; the ranking page should therefore rank by time-averaged premium over the window, and show 'no signal' when there's no liquidity ('if there is no price... there is no signal'). (4) Subsidized liquidity via an automated market maker — he invented LMSR for exactly this; a fixed per-market subsidy with extra liquidity near the measurement window is required, and pure orderbook/no-subsidy markets would earn his 2017 criticism; futarchy-fi's AMM approach qualifies if a sponsor funds the LP loss as an explicit information subsidy. (5) Insider participation design: anonymous trading for outsiders, disclosed trading for the CEO/insiders — approximable on-chain (pseudonymous by default; a voluntary 'disclosed insider' flag), and decision-selection-bias mitigations (announced decision windows; ideally a random-veto commitment if any decision linkage exists). (6) An audience strategy aimed at boards, activist investors, and media via a public cross-company premium ranking — this is the part NO past attempt delivered and where the product differentiates. What is NOT reusable/achievable on Sepolia: real-money incentives (testnet play money gives no information-revelation incentive, so Hanson would call it a demo of the mechanism, not the mechanism — frame it explicitly as a trial, which he endorses: 'concrete trials and experiments'); board commitments (no real board will bind to it — run it in his 'advisory futarchy' mode and build a track record); and real stock as numeraire (SEC derivative territory — must use a synthetic index of the actual share price via oracle, which changes the hedging economics and should be documented as a deviation). The 1996 dump-vs-spot single-market design is superseded — build the explicit pair, per his 2000+ writings.
risks: Decision selection bias / conditional-vs-causal gap: with purely advisory markets and better-informed boards, the fire premium can be biased (Hanson's own 2024-12-28 post and the dynomight critique he engaged); without announced decision windows or randomization the ranking measures correlation (bad-shape companies both fire CEOs and decline), and critics will attack the product on exactly this.

Thin/no liquidity: Hanson is explicit that no trading = no signal and that unsubsidized conditional markets stay empty; a 500-company ranking with unfunded LMSR subsidies produces noise premia that misrank companies and discredit the scoreboard.

Play-money testnet: no real stakes means no incentive to reveal information — Hanson's core mechanism is absent; over-claiming 'Hanson's vision, done' for a Sepolia demo invites his own 2017-style criticism of appearance-only markets.

Condition definition/oracle risk: 'CEO leaves by T' is endogenous and gameable (planned retirements, health exits, title shuffles to executive chairman); resolution rules must pre-specify what counts or premia become uninterpretable.

Legal: for the real-money version this is a security derivative (Hanson: 'You need some sort of permission there' re SEC/CFTC); even testnet marketing that implies tradable exposure to named public companies' stock needs care.

Manipulation defense assumes many competing traders and public knowledge of manipulation incentives; in tiny markets a motivated party (e.g., an activist fund talking its book, or the CEO's allies) can hold a premium at a chosen level for the whole averaging window.

Reputational: naming real CEOs on a public 'should be fired' ranking with thin play-money prices risks defamation-adjacent blowback and gives futarchy skeptics an easy target; Hanson's Power Futarchy post predicts CEOs will actively fight it rather than ignore it.
